topic: A person discovers the tree they planted as a child has been cut down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of damp earth and old bark hit Elias like a physical blow. He stood at the edge of the clearing, his hands gripping the rough bark of the remaining stump, knuckles white. Where a towering oak had stood just a few years ago, there was now only a jagged, splintered ruin, the evidence of recent, brutal violence. A tremor started deep in his chest, a frantic, uneven rhythm that made his breath catch in his throat. He sank to his knees, the cool mud seeping through his jeans, and stared at the devastation. All the hopeful afternoons spent coaxing that sapling into life, the whispered promises to the tiny green thing—it all collapsed into a suffocating weight. His vision blurred, and he could only manage a shallow, ragged inhale, the silence of the felled space amplifying the frantic drumming in his ears.

[story 2]

"No. No, this isn't right." Sarah whispered, her voice thin and reedy against the vast, empty expanse of the field. She walked the perimeter slowly, her footsteps crunching on dry leaves, each step seeming to drag through thick, unseen mud. Her shoulders slumped forward, a heavy curtain drawn across her frame. Her gaze kept flicking from the raw, exposed roots to the bare, scorched earth where the magnificent tree had anchored itself. A hot flush crept up her neck, making her skin prickle. She stopped abruptly, planting her feet wide, and let out a shaky exhale that sounded more like a sob caught in her throat. Her hands rose, trembling violently, as if trying to physically hold the reality of the destruction in place.

[story 3]

Mark circled the perimeter three times, his pace quickening with each revolution, a restless energy buzzing beneath his skin. He kept running a hand through his already disheveled hair, his jaw tight enough to crack. The sight of the stump—a stark, truncated monument to his youthful endeavor—made his stomach clench with a tight, nauseating twist. He stopped near the base, leaning his forehead against the cool wood, closing his eyes tightly. When he opened them again, the light seemed too bright, the sounds of the nearby woods too sharp. He pushed himself away from the tree with a sharp movement, his breathing coming in short, shallow gasps, his entire body vibrating with a profound sense of unraveling.
